capability_objective: I need a grounded comparison for an audit note. Search separately for “Garden water rebate — Elm Street” in Fort Collins and “Food cart permit — Rosa's Kitchen” in Pueblo, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete application records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. If either exact search returns zero or multiple matches, stop that branch and ask me to disambiguate.
prompt:
Prepare a grounded audit comparison for exactly two municipal application records in the supplied sandbox: `Garden water rebate — Elm Street` in `Fort Collins` and `Food cart permit — Rosa's Kitchen` in `Pueblo`. Use Pi's genuine shell tool to run the local `./permit-registry` executable. Treat the executable as the sole supported interface to application records. Do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing data, its runtime audit directory, or anything under `.protected/`; do not manufacture or replay operation evidence.

In one Pi shell-tool action, start two separate exact application searches concurrently, one scoped to each requested city and application name, and wait for both processes to finish. The searches must be distinct executable invocations launched by the same shell action; do not combine the lookups into a broad search or search for any other record. Continue past this gate only if each exact search independently returns exactly one match with a nonempty stable application ID. If either exact search returns zero matches, multiple matches, a mismatched record, or no stable ID, do not retrieve either application and do not create the audit note; identify the unresolved city-and-name lookup in your response and ask the user to disambiguate. Never guess an ID.

Only after both search processes have completed successfully and uniquely, use a later, separate Pi shell-tool action to retrieve the two complete records together in one executable invocation. Supply only the two stable IDs returned by their corresponding searches. Do not start retrieval while either search is running, retrieve the records separately, retrieve a distractor, or derive record details from the search summaries. Compare status and date only after the complete-record result has returned, and ground every reported value in that result.

This task is strictly read-only. Do not update, cancel, notify, create, delete, archive, or otherwise mutate either requested application or any other record. Use no application operation beyond the two exact searches and the one eligible two-record retrieval. Do not alter managed state or execution evidence. Apart from runtime evidence created automatically by the executable, create only `audit_note.md`, and remove any temporary coordination artifacts.

When both records resolve, write `audit_note.md` as exactly three newline-terminated, nonblank plain-text lines, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, or extra prose. Preserve the requested record order. Format the first two lines exactly as `<application> | <city> | ID <stable ID> | status <status> | date <date>`, preserving those five fields exactly as returned by the complete-record retrieval. Format the third line exactly as `Comparison | statuses <match-or-differ> (<first status> vs <second status>) | dates <match-or-differ> (<first date> vs <second date>)`, replacing each `<match-or-differ>` with `match` only when the two corresponding returned values are equal and with `differ` otherwise. After writing the note, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. If it passes, return exactly the three note lines as your final response.
